reading.passage:
By early 2026, the landscape of Vietnam's tourism industry underwent a radical transformation. Following a series of strategic investments in sustainable infrastructure, the country positioned itself as the premier "green-luxury" destination in Southeast Asia. The initiative, dubbed "Vision 2026," focused on decentralizing tourist hubs and promoting high-value experiences in previously underserved provinces such as Ha Giang and the Central Highlands.

One of the most significant shifts was the implementation of the Smart Visa 2.0 system, which utilized blockchain technology to streamline arrivals. This digital-first approach not only reduced processing times by 65% but also allowed for more granular data collection on traveler preferences. Consequently, the Vietnam National Authority of Tourism (VNAT) could tailor marketing campaigns to specific micro-segments, resulting in a 22% increase in the average length of stay.

Economic analysts have noted that the sector's contribution to the national GDP has reached an all-time high of 14.5%. This growth, however, has not come without challenges. The rapid expansion of eco-resorts has sparked debates regarding environmental conservation, prompting the government to enforce strict "net-zero" operational guidelines for all new developments within three years of opening.
reading.question: What was the primary focus of the "Vision 2026" initiative?
options: Expanding existing tourist hubs in major cities
Eliminating all luxury resorts for environmental reasons
Focusing exclusively on the Con Dao archipelago

reading.passage:
The commercial aviation industry is currently navigating one of its most transformative periods since the introduction of the jet engine. As airlines face mounting pressure to reduce carbon emissions and optimize operational efficiency, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a cornerstone of modernization strategies. By integrating machine learning algorithms into flight planning systems, airlines can now analyze complex meteorological data, air traffic patterns, and aircraft performance metrics in real-time. This capability allows for dynamic route adjustments, minimizing fuel consumption and significantly lowering greenhouse gas emissions.

Furthermore, predictive maintenance has revolutionized how airlines manage their fleets. Sensors embedded throughout the aircraft continuously monitor the health of critical components, transmitting data back to maintenance hubs. AI models predict potential failures before they occur, enabling proactive servicing. This shift from reactive to proactive maintenance has reduced aircraft downtime by an estimated 30% globally, directly improving flight punctuality and reducing passenger delays.

Despite these advancements, the transition to AI-driven operations presents substantial hurdles. The initial capital expenditure required to upgrade legacy systems is prohibitive for many smaller regional carriers. Additionally, there is a growing concern among aviation professionals regarding the potential deskilling of human operators as automation takes over increasingly complex tasks. Regulators are actively working to establish comprehensive frameworks to ensure that human oversight remains central to aviation safety protocols.
reading.question: Which of the following best describes the main idea of the passage?
options: The history of the jet engine in commercial aviation.
How predictive maintenance reduces aircraft downtime.
The transformative impact of AI on airline operations and its challenges.
The financial difficulties faced by regional carriers.

reading.passage: Vietravel has committed to reducing its carbon footprint by 30% by 2030. Key initiatives include transitioning to electric vehicles for city tours, partnering with eco-certified hotels, eliminating single-use plastics from all tour packages, and investing in carbon offset programs. In 2025, we achieved a 12% reduction through our Green Tourism initiative, which also improved customer satisfaction scores by 8%.

options: To inform guests about hotel services

writing.prompt: Write a short internal memo announcing a new expense policy: from December 1, all expense submissions exceeding 5,000,000 VND must have pre-approval from the immediate supervisor before the expense is incurred. Reference the Q2 internal audit recommendation, explain why the change is needed, and tell staff where to find the new approval form.

criteria.name: Task completion
criteria.description: Fully addresses the scenario including any nuance (apology, justification, next steps).

criteria.name: Grammar accuracy
criteria.description: Complex sentences, conditionals, passive voice used accurately at B2 level.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.description: Precise hospitality and business vocabulary; appropriate collocations.

criteria.name: Tone & coherence
criteria.description: Diplomatic, professional tone; clear discourse organization with transitions.

writing.prompt: Write a short letter from the Operations team to a tour partner who complained that two of our group bookings were cancelled with less than 48 hours' notice last month. Acknowledge the impact, summarise the corrective actions you have implemented (new approval workflow, weekly capacity review), and propose a meeting to rebuild confidence.

criteria.name: Task completion
criteria.description: Fully addresses the scenario including any nuance (apology, justification, next steps).

criteria.name: Grammar accuracy
criteria.description: Complex sentences, conditionals, passive voice used accurately at B2 level.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.description: Precise hospitality and business vocabulary; appropriate collocations.

criteria.name: Tone & coherence
criteria.description: Diplomatic, professional tone; clear discourse organization with transitions.

writing.prompt: Write a short HR briefing for the executive team on Q3 attrition. Q3 exit-interview data shows attrition is primarily driven by compensation gaps (45% of departures cited pay), with manager quality (22%) and career path (18%) secondary. Recommend a market-rate review for the three most-affected roles and a 12-month manager-coaching programme, and quantify the expected reduction in attrition (from 32% to 24% by end-2027).

criteria.name: Task completion
criteria.description: Comprehensive treatment of an analytical or persuasive task; supported reasoning.

criteria.name: Grammar & syntax
criteria.description: Wide range of structures handled accurately; few non-systematic errors.

criteria.name: Vocabulary precision
criteria.description: Precise business / management lexis; near-native collocation and register.

criteria.name: Argument & cohesion
criteria.description: Well-developed argument with cohesive devices, clear paragraphing, executive tone.

writing.prompt: Write a short formal response to the Compliance Committee's recent findings. Confirm that you have developed a remediation roadmap, that all high-risk items will be closed within 60 days (ahead of the regulatory 90-day deadline), and outline the governance: weekly status to the Committee chair and an independent verification by Internal Audit before sign-off.

criteria.name: Task completion
criteria.description: Comprehensive treatment of an analytical or persuasive task; supported reasoning.

criteria.name: Grammar & syntax
criteria.description: Wide range of structures handled accurately; few non-systematic errors.

criteria.name: Vocabulary precision
criteria.description: Precise business / management lexis; near-native collocation and register.

criteria.name: Argument & cohesion
criteria.description: Well-developed argument with cohesive devices, clear paragraphing, executive tone.

writing.prompt: Write a short board paper recommending an increase in cyber-insurance coverage from 200 billion VND to 400 billion VND. Justify the increase using the asymmetric risk profile evident in recent peer-event payouts (cite the range — 280–520 billion), quantify the marginal premium (~1.8 billion VND/year), and argue why failure to act would expose the balance sheet to existential risk in a worst-case ransomware scenario. Persuasive, balanced executive tone.

criteria.name: Task completion
criteria.description: Sophisticated executive-level communication with strategic framing and risk-aware reasoning.

criteria.name: Grammar & syntax
criteria.description: Mastery of complex syntax; essentially error-free.

criteria.name: Vocabulary precision
criteria.description: Idiomatic, domain-precise board-level vocabulary; nuanced register choices.

criteria.name: Argument & rhetoric
criteria.description: Sophisticated argument structure, balanced tone, persuasive without overstatement.

writing.prompt: Write a short formal letter to a coach-rental vendor whose on-time performance has fallen from 94% to 88% over the last quarter. State the breach of the 95% SLA, request a written improvement plan within 14 days covering the specific causes and corrective actions, and remind them that continued underperformance will trigger the contractual rebate clause.

writing.prompt: Write a short project status update for the steering committee on the booking-engine migration. Cover three things: (1) Phase 2 is complete and on schedule, (2) Phase 3 has been delayed by two weeks due to a third-party integration issue, and (3) the proposed mitigation is to begin Phase 4 work in parallel so the overall go-live date stays unchanged.

writing.prompt: Write a short proposal to the executive committee on a talent-retention initiative for high-performing front-line staff. Explain the problem (regrettable attrition in the top performance quintile is running at 22% annually), the proposed intervention (a 12-month rotation programme combining cross-functional exposure and a defined development budget per participant), and the expected outcome with rough cost framing.

writing.prompt: Write a short post-mortem summary for an incident in which the customer-facing payment page was unavailable for 47 minutes during a peak booking window. Include: a one-sentence incident description, the root cause (an expired TLS certificate on the third-party gateway), customer impact (estimated 320 abandoned bookings, ~580M VND in deferred revenue), and three concrete preventive actions with owners and deadlines.

writing.prompt: Write a short position paper for the CEO arguing for or against accepting a strategic-investment offer from a regional hotel group that would take a 25% minority stake in the company. Address: the strategic rationale, the principal risks (governance dilution, brand alignment, channel cannibalisation), the two or three deal terms that would have to be present for the offer to be acceptable, and your overall recommendation with the conditions under which you would revise it.
